mentions.note: Snack division struggling due to GLP-1 weight-loss drugs; CEO Raymon Magguire may take serious actions to reduce snack dependence; stock up $5 today on sector recovery.
Gave up post-earnings gains like classic 1999 market; stuck sideways until sentiment shifts favorably.
Stock went insane this week signaling recession fears; growth from snacking offset by pricing cuts; recession-proof proxy.
Consumer staple; company that makes products people understand; beneficiary of rotation from incomprehensible software.
February winner; defensive staples with strong brand prevailed during growth-stock rotation.
Stock rallied from $144 to $155 in seven sessions; strong move warrants waiting for earnings confirmation before buying.
Caller Bill holds 1,500 shares from 2008 at $40, now at $160 with 3.5% yield. CEO Ramon Laguarta executing well; hold despite war fears—don't sell quality on short-term uncertainty.

Second best packaged food company in world (after Coca-Cola); impressed by CEO Raml Magguire's navigation of processed food travails, GLP-1 weight loss drugs, healthy diet trends; demonstrates listening to customer.
Following General Mills bounce on packaged food narrative, but same structural headwinds apply; processed food foundation has crumbled.
